stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the field of text generation using transformer models.
Key technologies involved include Llama, transformers, and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking around Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: School shooting survivor sues AI gun detection firm after system failed to spot weapon
stories.author: Cyrus Farivar
bullet_points: A legal case has been filed against an AI gun detection firm following a school shooting.
The lawsuit alleges the AI system failed to detect a weapon in a critical situation.
The story raises significant ethical and practical questions regarding the required accuracy of AI safety systems.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now integrates MLX, Apple's native ML framework, for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets and optimizes the experience for Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI functionality for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking SpaceX from gaining easy access to passive investor capital.
The exclusion also affects other major AI players, specifically OpenAI and Anthropic.
This indicates a significant shift or hurdle in how large, high-growth tech companies are integrated into traditional investment indices.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Introduces Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework designed to inject repository-level context into Code Language Models.
Addresses the limitations of current methods (RAG/fine-tuning) which are costly or brittle when dealing with evolving codebases.
Achieves repository-specific knowledge injection without incurring any inference-time token overhead.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a new LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Aims to enable sustained self-evolution for complex, long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery and algorithm design.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for Code Language Models.
This method addresses the challenge of injecting large-scale repository context (imports, APIs) without incurring high computational costs.
It achieves knowledge injection with zero inference-time token overhead, making it robust to software evolution.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a new LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as inter-branch information isolation and memoryless search.
Aims to enable sustained, long-horizon optimization necessary for complex tasks like scientific discovery and algorithm design.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB speaker device has been found capable of infecting a PC without physical user interaction.
The specific device mentioned is the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The seller of the device does not classify the observed behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A startup named Antares has achieved criticality in a small modular nuclear reactor (SMR).
The reactor is currently in an early testing phase and is not yet capable of generating power.
This development represents progress in the field of advanced nuclear energy technology.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in humanoid robotics: bridging the gap between high-level task planning and low-level whole-body control.
Introduces HANDOFF, a novel framework designed to provide a compact, explicit, and intuitive interface between these two domains.
The work focuses on enabling diverse manipulation skills by moving away from traditional, dense kinematic references.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed.
The research tackles the need for dynamic speed control in robot manipulation, distinguishing between fast transit and slow, precise contact phases.
Unlike prior methods that only shift between fixed speeds, TempoVLA specifically explores and enables effective deceleration in VLA policies.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the process, not just the final output.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during human-AI co-editing.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential computation and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT achieves this by completely bypassing the need for recurrent credit propagation, potentially improving parallelism and long-range dependency learning.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work focuses on overcoming limitations of standard Monte Carlo methods like GRPO when training Chain-of-Thought (CoT) models.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: Introduces a novel technique, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by iteratively denoising the entire response in parallel, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens from the denoising process are shown to serve as a useful lookahead signal for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows for merging preconditioned weights back into the original architecture with zero inference overhead.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
It uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame for low-rank updates.
A soft spectral penalty is employed to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, focusing adaptation on 'long-tail' features.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Proposes HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control using a compact interface.
Utilizes distilled Complementary Teachers to achieve an intuitive, general, and expressive control interface.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new benchmark designed to study the evolution of AI authorship signals.
Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection, which typically only analyzes final outputs rather than the co-editing process.
Focuses on 'progressive human-AI co-editing' workflows, reflecting the increasing integration of AI assistants in real-world drafting.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs).
Proposes Supervised Memory Training (SMT) to bypass the need for recurrent credit propagation.
Aims to improve the learning of long-range associations by overcoming vanishing/exploding gradient issues.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve RL training for Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces.
The work targets limitations in existing methods like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) which rely on Monte Carlo-style delayed feedback.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models.
It leverages 'discarded' low-confidence tokens from the parallel denoising process as a valuable lookahead signal.
This signal enhances Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by enabling the retrieval of stronger evidence during text generation.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer for Large Language Model (LLM) pre-training.
The PC layer stabilizes weight conditioning by reshaping the singular-value spectrum using low-degree polynomials.
It offers performance benefits over standard transformers without adding inference overhead, as weights can be merged post-training.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the *process* of human-AI co-editing, not just the final output.
Introduces 'OpAI-Bench,' a new operation-guided benchmark designed to track how AI authorship signals change during revision workflows.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants in professional drafting and revision processes.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by iteratively denoising the entire response in parallel, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens from the denoising process are shown to act as a valuable lookahead signal for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses the efficiency bottleneck in long-context inference for LLMs, particularly in reasoning tasks.
It tackles the common trade-off between speed and quality in existing sparse attention methods.
The proposed 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing' aims to improve efficiency without significant quality degradation.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the effectiveness of 'prompt skills' (like asking an LLM to reason as a Popperian falsificationist) in improving code generation by LLMs.
A key concern raised is that reported gains are often measured using LLM-as-a-judge systems, which suffer from known biases (positional, self-preference, stylistic).
The research questions whether the observed performance improvements are genuine gains in coding ability or artifacts of the evaluation methodology.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations of Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs, noting that verbalizing reasoning steps is restrictive.
It proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as a high-bandwidth alternative to textual CoT.
Latent reasoning allows for intermediate computation that is semantic or uncertain, rather than being forced through a discrete token stream.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs.
Addresses the limitations of current domain-specific audio encoders by integrating knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods.
Highlights the growing trend of LLMs relying on unified encoders for various input types, emphasizing the need for multi-domain audio understanding.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of using LLMs to simulate user stances in online discussions.
It introduces 'counterfactual context revision' as a framework to audit these LLM simulations.
The study investigates whether LLM simulations accurately reflect user beliefs or are overly sensitive to minor context changes.

stories.title: Speculative KV coding: losslessly compressing KV cache by up to ~4×
stories.author: kkm
bullet_points: The article discusses speculative KV coding, a technique for compressing the Key-Value (KV) cache.
This compression method claims to achieve lossless compression of the KV cache by up to 4x.
This is relevant to optimizing large language model (LLM) inference efficiency.

stories.title: Run Coding Agents on Local AI — Zero Cloud, Full Control
stories.author: dalenguyen
bullet_points: The article discusses the use of coding agents (like Codex CLI, Claude Code, Cursor, Pi) as productivity tools.
The central theme is running these coding agents locally, emphasizing 'Zero Cloud' operation.
This local execution offers users 'Full Control' over their AI coding assistants.

stories.title: Free Video subtitles in seconds — no signup, no watermark, no upload
stories.author: captionly
bullet_points: A new tool, Captionly, has been developed to generate video subtitles quickly.
The application utilizes the Whisper model and runs entirely within the user's browser.
Key features include no required signup, no watermarks, and no file uploads.

stories.title: I ran an fMRI on LLMs: a concept is a direction, not a region
stories.author: jskim_7a1f310dceb06a5ebb1
bullet_points: The article discusses an experimental approach termed 'fMRI for LLMs'.
This method involves capturing the full internal activations of Large Language Models.
The core concept suggests that understanding LLM function is about directionality rather than localized regions.

stories.title: Turning Your AI Into an Adversarial Security Agent: The SKILLS.md Framework
stories.author: shubham399
bullet_points: Introduces the SKILLS.md framework for developing AI into adversarial security agents.
Connects AI development with concepts from CTF and Bug Bounty hunting methodologies.
Focuses on the intersection of AI/ML and offensive cybersecurity practices.
